key_insight: For RLVR, orthonormal low-rank initialization best preserves the full-fine-tuning geometry, reducing the gap between LoRA and full adaptation and improving stability.
engineering_relevance: The result gives a practical initialization rule for parameter-efficient RL training on math-reasoning tasks, where some SFT-strong LoRA variants can become unstable or underperform.
method: Analyze LoRA behavior under reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR).
Show theoretically that orthonormal initialization minimizes the LoRA-vs-full-finetuning outcome gap.
Introduce geometry-preserving orthonormal variants: RLPO and RLMO.
evidence_signal: Experiments on mathematical reasoning benchmarks show the proposed orthonormal initialization stabilizes RLVR training and outperforms standard LoRA, while PiSSA and MiLoRA can underperform.
watch_item: The gains are demonstrated in RLVR math-reasoning settings, so the initialization may need revalidation for other tasks, reward designs, or model scales.
paper_title: Geometry-Preserving Orthonormal Initialization for Low-Rank Adaptation in RLVR
